title: FORGE Session Handoff
description: Machine-parseable session handoff artifact for automated context transfer between sessions and agents. Generated as a JSON sidecar alongside each markdown session log.

session_id.description: Session identifier matching the log filename (e.g., 2026-03-27-001).

summary.description: 2-3 sentence summary: goal, what was completed, what is deferred.

decisions.description: Concrete choices about code, schema, process, or architecture made this session.

description.description: Self-contained, searchable description of the decision.

specs_touched.description: Specs created, modified, or referenced this session.

status.description: Spec status at end of session.

action.description: What was done with this spec during the session.

gate.description: Gate name or type (e.g., implementation-gate, validation-gate).

notes.description: Additional context about the gate outcome.

open_items.description: Unresolved items carried forward: spec triggers, pain points, process improvements.

spec_id.description: Related spec ID, if one was created.

next_actions.description: Recommended actions for the next session, in priority order.

error_autopsies.description: Error autopsies created this session (EA-NNN entries).

root_cause_category.description: Spec 267 — taxonomy bucket for downstream pattern analysis. Absent fields are treated as 'other' (backward-compat with pre-Spec-267 entries).

wrong_assumption.description: Spec 267 — the specific belief held before the bug surfaced, now known to be false. Empty string when the error wasn't an assumption failure.

evidence_gate_coverage.description: Spec 267 — whether an existing evidence gate caught this, missed this, or no gate applies. When 'missed-by-existing-gate', the named gate appears in the markdown entry.

chat_insights.description: Chat insights recorded this session (CI-NNN entries).

root_cause_category.description: Spec 267 — taxonomy bucket for downstream pattern analysis. Absent fields are treated as 'other' (backward-compat with pre-Spec-267 entries).

wrong_assumption.description: Spec 267 — the specific belief held before the insight surfaced, now known to be false. Empty string when the insight is not about an assumption failure.

evidence_gate_coverage.description: Spec 267 — whether an existing evidence gate caught this, missed this, or no gate applies. When 'missed-by-existing-gate', the named gate appears in the markdown entry.

consensus_reviews.description: Spec 258/495 — per-/consensus-invocation acceptance records. Canonical source for the rolling-30-day consensus acceptance rate (accepted / (accepted + modified + rejected)) surfaced by /now and /evolve F4 (read side wired by Spec 497). 'consensus_reviews' is the canonical field name (what the /close writer emits); the Spec 258 prose name 'consensus_outcomes' is an accepted alias for historical sidecars.

operator_decision.description: accepted | modified (accepted-with-revisions) | rejected | <free-text disposition>. The acceptance-rate classifier buckets on the leading token (accepted/modified/rejected).

consensus_outcomes.description: Spec 495 — alias of consensus_reviews (the Spec 258 prose name). Accepted so historical sidecars that used this name still validate; new writers emit consensus_reviews. Same permissive item shape.

token_usage.description: Spec 497 / ADR-496 — per-session token usage captured by parsing the on-disk transcript JSONL (transcript-parse, token-only). Optional and additive. Stores TOKEN COUNTS ONLY; per ADR-316 + ADR-496 no cost (USD) figure is ever stored. The capture point is end-of-/implement (written to the per-spec event stream) folded into this durable field by /session.

total_tokens.description: Sum of the four movement fields.
